11	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
12	// Module-level helpers
13	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
14	
15	/**
16	 * Map API column value → state key.
17	 * 'in-progress' (hyphen) → 'in_progress' (underscore); others pass through.
18	 * @param {string} col
19	 * @returns {string}
20	 */
21	export function columnToKey(col) {
22	  return col === 'in-progress' ? 'in_progress' : col
23	}
24	
25	/**
26	 * Group a flat array of cards into the board state shape, sorted by position.
27	 * @param {import('../api/client.js').Card[]} cards
28	 * @returns {{ ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] }}
29	 */
30	function groupCards(cards) {
31	  const board = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
32	  for (const card of cards) {
33	    const key = columnToKey(card.column)
34	    if (key in board) board[key].push(card)
35	  }
36	  for (const key of Object.keys(board)) {
37	    board[key].sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
38	  }
39	  return board
40	}
41	
42	const EMPTY_BOARD = { ready: [], in_progress: [], done: [] }
43	
44	// Temp-ID counter for optimistic records (module-level so it's stable across renders)
45	let _tempId = 0
46	function nextTempId() {
47	  return '__temp_' + (++_tempId)
48	}
49	
50	// WebSocket constants (module-level — computed once on import)
51	const WS_URL = `${location.protocol === 'https:' ? 'wss:' : 'ws:'}//${location.host}/ws`
52	const WS_EVENTS = ['card:created', 'card:updated', 'card:deleted', 'card:moved', 'comment:created']
53	
54	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
55	// useBoard hook
56	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	
58	/**
59	 * Manages board state: cards grouped by column, loading, and error.
60	 *
61	 * Column convention
62	 *   - State keys:  ready | in_progress | done  (underscore)
63	 *   - API values:  ready | in-progress  | done  (hyphen for in-progress)
64	 *   - All hook functions that accept a column name expect the API format.
65	 *   - Each card's .column field retains the API format.
66	 *
67	 * @returns {{
68	 *   cards: { ready: Card[], in_progress: Card[], done: Card[] },
69	 *   loading: boolean,
70	 *   error: string|null,
71	 *   createCard: (data: object) => Promise<Card>,
72	 *   updateCard: (id: string, data: object) => Promise<Card>,
73	 *   deleteCard: (id: string) => Promise<void>,
74	 *   moveCard: (id: string, targetColumn: string, position?: number) => Promise<Card>,
75	 *   addComment: (cardId: string, data: object) => Promise<Comment>,
76	 * }}
77	 */
78	export function useBoard() {
79	  const [cards, setCards] = useState(EMPTY_BOARD)
80	  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false)
81	  const [error, setError] = useState(null)
82	
83	  // Counter for concurrent in-flight operations so loading stays true
84	  // until the LAST operation finishes.
85	  const pendingRef = useRef(0)
86	
87	  // Ref that always holds the latest cards state so we can read it
88	  // synchronously (functional updaters are called lazily by React, which
89	  // would leave a rollback variable null if the API rejects as a microtask
90	  // before the updater runs).
91	  const cardsRef = useRef(EMPTY_BOARD)
92	
93	  // Suppression map — consume-once with 5s TTL to ignore WS echo of local actions
94	  const suppressedRef = useRef(new Map())
95	
96	  // Tracks whether we've had at least one successful connection (for reconnect detection)
97	  const hasConnectedRef = useRef(false)
98	
99	  /**
100	   * Apply a state update eagerly: compute the next value synchronously from
101	   * cardsRef.current, update the ref, then call setCards with the plain
102	   * computed value (not a function) so React picks it up on the next render.
103	   */
104	  function applyCards(updater) {
105	    const next = typeof updater === 'function' ? updater(cardsRef.current) : updater
106	    cardsRef.current = next
107	    setCards(next)
108	  }
109	
110	  function beginOp() { pendingRef.current++; setLoading(true) }
111	  function endOp()   { if (--pendingRef.current === 0) setLoading(false) }
112	
113	  function suppressWsEvent(id) {
114	    suppressedRef.current.set(id, Date.now() + 5000)
115	  }
116	
117	  function consumeSuppression(id) {
118	    const expiry = suppressedRef.current.get(id)
119	    if (expiry === undefined) return false
120	    suppressedRef.current.delete(id)
121	    return Date.now() <= expiry
122	  }
123	
124	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	  // WebSocket event handler (stub — filled in Subtask 2)
126	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	
128	  // useCallback([]) is safe: applyCards/consumeSuppression close only over stable refs
129	  const handleWsEvent = useCallback((eventType, payload) => {
130	    switch (eventType) {
131	      case 'card:created': {
132	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
133	        const key = columnToKey(payload.column)
134	        applyCards(prev => {
135	          if (prev[key].some(c => c.id === payload.id)) return prev
136	          return {
137	            ...prev,
138	            [key]: [...prev[key], { ...payload, comments: payload.comments ?? [] }]
139	              .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position),
140	          }
141	        })
142	        break
143	      }
144	      case 'card:updated': {
145	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
146	        // Remove from current column, re-insert in correct column.
147	        // Handles both in-place updates AND cross-column moves via PATCH /api/cards/:id.
148	        // Preserves existing comments (WS payload does not include comments array).
149	        const newKey = columnToKey(payload.column)
150	        applyCards(prev => {
151	          let existingComments = []
152	          let found = false
153	          const next = {}
154	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
155	            const card = col.find(c => c.id === payload.id)
156	            if (card) { existingComments = card.comments ?? []; found = true }
157	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
158	          }
159	          if (!found) return prev
160	          next[newKey] = [...next[newKey], { ...payload, comments: existingComments }]
161	            .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
162	          return next
163	        })
164	        break
165	      }
166	      case 'card:deleted': {
167	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
168	        applyCards(prev => {
169	          const next = {}
170	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
171	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
172	          }
173	          return next
174	        })
175	        break
176	      }
177	      case 'card:moved': {
178	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
179	        const newKey = columnToKey(payload.column)
180	        applyCards(prev => {
181	          const next = {}
182	          let existingComments = []
183	          let found = false
184	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
185	            const card = col.find(c => c.id === payload.id)
186	            if (card) { existingComments = card.comments ?? []; found = true }
187	            next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== payload.id)
188	          }
189	          if (!found) return prev
190	          next[newKey] = [...next[newKey], { ...payload, comments: existingComments }]
191	            .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
192	          return next
193	        })
194	        break
195	      }
196	      case 'comment:created': {
197	        if (consumeSuppression(payload.id)) break
198	        applyCards(prev => {
199	          const next = {}
200	          for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
201	            next[k] = col.map(c =>
202	              c.id === payload.card_id
203	                ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, payload] }
204	                : c
205	            )
206	          }
207	          return next
208	        })
209	        break
210	      }
211	    }
212	  }, [])
213	
214	  const { status: wsStatus } = useWebSocket(WS_URL, { onEvent: handleWsEvent, events: WS_EVENTS })
215	
216	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
217	  // Reconnect reconciliation — re-fetch on WS reconnect to recover missed events
218	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
219	
220	  useEffect(() => {
221	    if (wsStatus === 'connected') {
222	      if (hasConnectedRef.current) {
223	        // Reconnect detected — re-fetch to reconcile any missed events
224	        let cancelled = false
225	        fetchCards()
226	          .then(data => { if (!cancelled) applyCards(groupCards(data)) })
227	          .catch(() => {}) // silent fail; don't disrupt existing state
228	        return () => { cancelled = true }
229	      }
230	      hasConnectedRef.current = true
231	    }
232	  }, [wsStatus])
233	
234	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
235	  // Initial data load
236	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
237	
238	  useEffect(() => {
239	    let cancelled = false
240	    setError(null)
241	    beginOp()
242	
243	    fetchCards()
244	      .then(data => {
245	        if (cancelled) return
246	        applyCards(groupCards(data))
247	        endOp()
248	      })
249	      .catch(err => {
250	        if (cancelled) return
251	        setError(err.message)
252	        endOp()
253	      })
254	
255	    return () => { cancelled = true }
256	  }, [])
257	
258	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
259	  // createCard — temp-ID optimistic pattern
260	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
261	
262	  const createCard = useCallback(async (data) => {
263	    const tempId = nextTempId()
264	    const col = data.column ?? 'ready'
265	    const key = columnToKey(col)
266	
267	    const optimisticCard = {
268	      id: tempId,
269	      title: data.title,
270	      assignee: data.assignee ?? null,
271	      column: col,
272	      position: Infinity,
273	      description: data.description ?? null,
274	      created_at: Date.now(),
275	      comments: [],
276	    }
277	
278	    applyCards(prev => ({ ...prev, [key]: [...prev[key], optimisticCard] }))
279	    beginOp()
280	
281	    try {
282	      const created = await apiCreateCard(data)
283	      suppressWsEvent(created.id)
284	      const createdKey = columnToKey(created.column)
285	      applyCards(prev => {
286	        const next = { ...prev }
287	        // Remove the temp card from whichever column it was placed in
288	        next[key] = next[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId)
289	        // Insert the server card into the column the server assigned.
290	        // Filter out any WS-added duplicate (card:created can arrive before the
291	        // HTTP 201 response when broadcast precedes the response flush).
292	        next[createdKey] = [...next[createdKey].filter(c => c.id !== created.id), created]
293	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
294	        return next
295	      })
296	      return created
297	    } catch (err) {
298	      applyCards(prev => ({
299	        ...prev,
300	        [key]: prev[key].filter(c => c.id !== tempId),
301	      }))
302	      throw err
303	    } finally {
304	      endOp()
305	    }
306	  }, [])
307	
308	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
309	  // updateCard — rollback pattern (column changes ignored; use moveCard)
310	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
311	
312	  const updateCard = useCallback(async (id, data) => {
313	    // Strip column — column changes must go through moveCard
314	    const safeData = { ...data }
315	    delete safeData.column
316	
317	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref (not inside a lazy updater)
318	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
319	    suppressWsEvent(id)
320	    applyCards(prev => {
321	      const next = {}
322	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
323	        next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...c, ...safeData } : c)
324	      }
325	      return next
326	    })
327	    beginOp()
328	
329	    try {
330	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, safeData)
331	      applyCards(prev => {
332	        const next = {}
333	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
334	          // Preserve existing comments — PATCH response doesn't include them
335	          next[k] = col.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...updated, comments: c.comments ?? [] } : c)
336	        }
337	        return next
338	      })
339	      return updated
340	    } catch (err) {
341	      applyCards(rollback)
342	      throw err
343	    } finally {
344	      endOp()
345	    }
346	  }, [])
347	
348	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
349	  // deleteCard — rollback pattern
350	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
351	
352	  const deleteCard = useCallback(async (id) => {
353	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
354	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
355	    suppressWsEvent(id)
356	    applyCards(prev => {
357	      const next = {}
358	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
359	        next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
360	      }
361	      return next
362	    })
363	    beginOp()
364	
365	    try {
366	      await apiDeleteCard(id)
367	    } catch (err) {
368	      applyCards(rollback)
369	      throw err
370	    } finally {
371	      endOp()
372	    }
373	  }, [])
374	
375	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
376	  // moveCard — rollback pattern
377	  // targetColumn must be in API format ('ready' | 'in-progress' | 'done')
378	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
379	
380	  const moveCard = useCallback(async (id, targetColumn, position) => {
381	    const targetKey = columnToKey(targetColumn)
382	
383	    // Capture rollback synchronously from the ref
384	    const rollback = cardsRef.current
385	    suppressWsEvent(id)
386	    applyCards(prev => {
387	      let cardToMove = null
388	      const next = {}
389	
390	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
391	        const idx = col.findIndex(c => c.id === id)
392	        if (idx !== -1) {
393	          cardToMove = col[idx]
394	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
395	        } else {
396	          next[k] = col
397	        }
398	      }
399	
400	      if (!cardToMove) return prev
401	
402	      const movedCard = {
403	        ...cardToMove,
404	        column: targetColumn,
405	        ...(position !== undefined ? { position } : {}),
406	      }
407	      next[targetKey] = [...next[targetKey], movedCard]
408	        .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
409	      return next
410	    })
411	    beginOp()
412	
413	    try {
414	      const payload = { column: targetColumn }
415	      if (position !== undefined) payload.position = position
416	      const updated = await apiUpdateCard(id, payload)
417	      const updatedKey = columnToKey(updated.column)
418	      applyCards(prev => {
419	        const next = {}
420	        let existingComments = []
421	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
422	          const card = col.find(c => c.id === id)
423	          if (card) existingComments = card.comments ?? []
424	          next[k] = col.filter(c => c.id !== id)
425	        }
426	        next[updatedKey] = [...next[updatedKey], { ...updated, comments: existingComments }]
427	          .sort((a, b) => a.position - b.position)
428	        return next
429	      })
430	      return updated
431	    } catch (err) {
432	      applyCards(rollback)
433	      throw err
434	    } finally {
435	      endOp()
436	    }
437	  }, [])
438	
439	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
440	  // addComment — temp-ID optimistic pattern
441	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
442	
443	  const addComment = useCallback(async (cardId, data) => {
444	    const tempId = nextTempId()
445	    const optimisticComment = {
446	      id: tempId,
447	      card_id: cardId,
448	      author: data.author,
449	      content: data.content,
450	      created_at: Date.now(),
451	    }
452	
453	    applyCards(prev => {
454	      const next = {}
455	      for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
456	        next[k] = col.map(c =>
457	          c.id === cardId
458	            ? { ...c, comments: [...c.comments, optimisticComment] }
459	            : c
460	        )
461	      }
462	      return next
463	    })
464	    beginOp()
465	
466	    try {
467	      const comment = await createComment(cardId, data)
468	      suppressWsEvent(comment.id)
469	      applyCards(prev => {
470	        const next = {}
471	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
472	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
473	            c.id === cardId
474	              ? {
475	                  ...c,
476	                  // Filter out both the temp comment and any WS-added real comment
477	                  // (comment:created can arrive before the HTTP 201 response)
478	                  comments: c.comments
479	                    .filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId && cm.id !== comment.id)
480	                    .concat(comment),
481	                }
482	              : c
483	          )
484	        }
485	        return next
486	      })
487	      return comment
488	    } catch (err) {
489	      applyCards(prev => {
490	        const next = {}
491	        for (const [k, col] of Object.entries(prev)) {
492	          next[k] = col.map(c =>
493	            c.id === cardId
494	              ? { ...c, comments: c.comments.filter(cm => cm.id !== tempId) }
495	              : c
496	          )
497	        }
498	        return next
499	      })
500	      throw err
501	    } finally {
502	      endOp()
503	    }
504	  }, [])
505	
506	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
507	  // Public interface
508	  // -------------------------------------------------------------------------
509	
510	  return {
511	    cards,
512	    loading,
513	    error,
514	    createCard,
515	    updateCard,
516	    deleteCard,
517	    moveCard,
518	    addComment,
519	  }
520	}

1	/**
2	 * API client for the Kanban board REST API.
3	 * All functions use relative URLs so Vite's dev proxy (/api → localhost:3001)
4	 * and same-origin production serving both work without configuration.
5	 */
6	
7	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
8	// Type definitions
9	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	
11	/**
12	 * @typedef {Object} Card
13	 * @property {string} id
14	 * @property {string} title
15	 * @property {string|null} assignee
16	 * @property {string} column - e.g. 'ready', 'in-progress', 'done'
17	 * @property {number} position
18	 * @property {string|null} description
19	 * @property {number} created_at - Unix ms timestamp
20	 * @property {Comment[]} comments
21	 */
22	
23	/**
24	 * @typedef {Object} Comment
25	 * @property {string} id
26	 * @property {string} card_id
27	 * @property {string} author
28	 * @property {string} content
29	 * @property {number} created_at
30	 */
31	
32	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
33	// ApiError
34	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
35	
36	export class ApiError extends Error {
37	  /**
38	   * @param {string} msg
39	   * @param {number} status  HTTP status code, or 0 for network-level failures
40	   * @param {*} data         Parsed error response body, or null
41	   */
42	  constructor(msg, status, data) {
43	    super(msg)
44	    this.name = 'ApiError'
45	    this.status = status
46	    this.data = data
47	  }
48	}
49	
50	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
51	// Internal fetch helper
52	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
53	
54	async function apiFetch(path, options = {}) {
55	  const headers = {}
56	  if (options.body !== undefined) headers['Content-Type'] = 'application/json'
57	
58	  let response
59	  try {
60	    response = await fetch(path, { ...options, headers: { ...headers, ...options.headers } })
61	  } catch (err) {
62	    throw new ApiError(`Network error: ${err.message}`, 0, null)
63	  }
64	
65	  if (!response.ok) {
66	    let errorData = null
67	    try { errorData = await response.json() } catch { /* ignore */ }
68	    const message = errorData?.error ?? `HTTP error ${response.status}`
69	    throw new ApiError(message, response.status, errorData)
70	  }
71	
72	  if (response.status === 204 || response.headers.get('content-length') === '0') {
73	    return null
74	  }
75	
76	  try {
77	    return await response.json()
78	  } catch (err) {
79	    throw new ApiError(`Failed to parse response: ${err.message}`, response.status, null)
80	  }
81	}
82	
83	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
84	// Public API functions
85	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
86	
87	/**
88	 * Fetch all cards with their nested comments.
89	 * @returns {Promise<Card[]>}
90	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
91	 * @example
92	 * const cards = await fetchCards()
93	 * console.log(cards[0].title)
94	 */
95	export async function fetchCards() {
96	  return apiFetch('/api/cards')
97	}
98	
99	/**
100	 * Create a new card.
101	 * @param {{ title: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string }} data
102	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
103	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
104	 * @example
105	 * const card = await createCard({ title: 'Fix bug', assignee: 'Alice' })
106	 * console.log(card.id)
107	 */
108	export async function createCard(data) {
109	  return apiFetch('/api/cards', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
110	}
111	
112	/**
113	 * Partially update an existing card.
114	 * @param {string} id
115	 * @param {{ title?: string, assignee?: string, column?: string, description?: string, position?: number }} data
116	 * @returns {Promise<Card>}
117	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
118	 * @example
119	 * const updated = await updateCard('abc-123', { column: 'done' })
120	 */
121	export async function updateCard(id, data) {
122	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
123	}
124	
125	/**
126	 * Delete a card and all its comments.
127	 * @param {string} id
128	 * @returns {Promise<null>}
129	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
130	 * @example
131	 * await deleteCard('abc-123')
132	 */
133	export async function deleteCard(id) {
134	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${id}`, { method: 'DELETE' })
135	}
136	
137	/**
138	 * Add a comment to a card.
139	 * @param {string} cardId
140	 * @param {{ author: string, content: string }} data
141	 * @returns {Promise<Comment>}
142	 * @throws {ApiError} on network or HTTP error
143	 * @example
144	 * const comment = await createComment('abc-123', { author: 'Bob', content: 'Looks good!' })
145	 */
146	export async function createComment(cardId, data) {
147	  return apiFetch(`/api/cards/${cardId}/comments`, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })
148	}

1	import { useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback } from 'react'
2	
3	const INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS = 1000
4	const MAX_BACKOFF_MS = 30_000
5	
6	/**
7	 * @param {string} url - WebSocket server URL
8	 * @param {object} [options]
9	 * @param {(eventType: string, payload: object) => void} [options.onEvent]
10	 * @param {(error: Error) => void} [options.onError]
11	 * @param {string[]} [options.events] - optional allowlist of event type strings
12	 * @returns {{ status: 'connecting'|'connected'|'disconnected'|'error', disconnect: () => void }}
13	 */
14	export function useWebSocket(url, { onEvent, onError, events } = {}) {
15	  const [status, setStatus] = useState('connecting')
16	
17	  const wsRef                = useRef(null)
18	  const intentionalCloseRef  = useRef(false)
19	  const reconnectTimerRef    = useRef(null)
20	  const reconnectAttemptsRef = useRef(0)
21	  const onEventRef           = useRef(onEvent)
22	  const onErrorRef           = useRef(onError)
23	  const eventsRef            = useRef(events)
24	
25	  // Keep callback/filter refs current on every render (direct assignment — no reconnect side effects)
26	  onEventRef.current = onEvent
27	  onErrorRef.current = onError
28	  eventsRef.current  = events
29	
30	  useEffect(() => {
31	    intentionalCloseRef.current = false
32	    reconnectAttemptsRef.current = 0 // reset on URL change or initial mount
33	
34	    function connect() {
35	      setStatus('connecting')
36	      const ws = new WebSocket(url)
37	      wsRef.current = ws
38	
39	      ws.onopen = () => {
40	        reconnectAttemptsRef.current = 0
41	        setStatus('connected')
42	      }
43	
44	      ws.onclose = () => {
45	        wsRef.current = null
46	        setStatus('disconnected')
47	        if (intentionalCloseRef.current) return
48	        // Guard against multiple simultaneous timers
49	        clearTimeout(reconnectTimerRef.current)
50	        const delay = Math.min(
51	          INITIAL_BACKOFF_MS * Math.pow(2, reconnectAttemptsRef.current),
52	          MAX_BACKOFF_MS
53	        )
54	        reconnectAttemptsRef.current += 1
55	        reconnectTimerRef.current = setTimeout(connect, delay)
56	      }
57	
58	      ws.onerror = () => {
59	        setStatus('error')
60	        onErrorRef.current?.(new Error('WebSocket connection error'))
61	      }
62	
63	      ws.onmessage = ({ data }) => {
64	        try {
65	          const { event: eventType, payload } = JSON.parse(data)
66	          // Skip if no event type
67	          if (!eventType) return
68	          // Skip if event filter is active and this type is not included
69	          if (eventsRef.current && !eventsRef.current.includes(eventType)) return
70	          onEventRef.current?.(eventType, payload)
71	        } catch (err) {
72	          onErrorRef.current?.(err)
73	        }
74	      }
75	    }
76	
77	    connect()
78	
79	    return () => {
80	      intentionalCloseRef.current = true
81	      clearTimeout(reconnectTimerRef.current)
82	      wsRef.current?.close()
83	      wsRef.current = null
84	    }
85	  }, [url])
86	
87	  const disconnect = useCallback(() => {
88	    intentionalCloseRef.current = true
89	    clearTimeout(reconnectTimerRef.current)
90	    wsRef.current?.close()
91	    setStatus('disconnected')
92	  }, [])
93	
94	  return { status, disconnect }
95	}

content.text:
Now I have everything I need to produce a comprehensive implementation plan. Here it is: --- # TDD Implementation Plan: Production Optimizations and Error Handling ## Preamble: Conventions Observed in the Codebase Before the subtask plans, several conventions must be respected throughout: - **Server tests**: `.mjs` files in `server/test/`, using `node:test` + `node:assert/strict` + native `fetch`. No `supertest`. Each suite manages its own `startTestServer` / `stopServer` helpers (defined locally in the file). Tests run with `node --test`. - **Server modules**: ESM (`type: "module"`). All new server files use `import`/`export`, never `require`. - **Client tests**: `.test.jsx` or `.test.js` in co-located `src/` directories, using Vitest + React Testing Library. `vi.mock()` for all hooks and heavy dependencies. - **Error response shape**: existing routes return `{ error: string }`. The plan enriches this to `{ error: string, code: string, details?: any }` while keeping the `error` field, so all existing test assertions (`assert.strictEqual(typeof body.error, 'string')`) continue to pass. - **Middleware directory**: `server/middleware/` does not yet exist and must be created as part of the work. --- ## Execution Order and Dependencies ``` Subtask 1 (Error Handler Middleware) → Subtask 2 (Morgan / Logger) depends on S1 being stable → Subtask 3 (Rate Limiting / Helmet) depends on S1 and S2 being in place → Subtask 6 (CORS / Health Checks) is partially independent; DB health check needs Subtask 1 done Subtask 4 (Vite Code Splitting) — fully independent of S1–S3 Subtask 5 (React Error Boundaries) — fully independent of S1–S4, but logically pairs with S4 ``` Recommended order: **1 → 2 → 3 → 6 → 4 → 5** Subtasks 4 and 5 are purely client-side and can be done in parallel with the server subtasks if there are multiple contributors. --- ## New Packages to Install ### Server (`kanban/server/`) ``` npm -w server install morgan express-rate-limit helmet ``` Add to `server/package.json` `dependencies`: - `morgan` — HTTP request logger middleware - `express-rate-limit` — Rate limiting - `helmet` — Security headers No new `devDependencies` needed for the server; the existing `node:test` + native `fetch` covers all new tests. ### Client (`kanban/client/`) No new packages needed. `React.lazy` + `Suspense` are built into React 18. The `ErrorBoundary` is a class component using existing React APIs. --- ## Subtask 1: Comprehensive Error Handling Middleware ### New files - `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` — the error handler middleware - `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` — the test suite ### Test file: `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` Structure using `node:test`: ``` describe('errorHandler middleware — unit') it('maps NotFoundError to 404 with code NOT_FOUND') it('maps ForeignKeyError to 404 with code NOT_FOUND') it('maps DatabaseError to 500 with code DATABASE_ERROR') it('maps validation error (err.status=400) to 400 with code VALIDATION_ERROR') it('maps auth error (err.status=401) to 401 with code UNAUTHORIZED') it('maps auth error (err.status=403) to 403 with code FORBIDDEN') it('maps generic Error to 500 with code INTERNAL_ERROR') it('response body always has { error: string, code: string } shape') it('includes stack trace in response details when NODE_ENV=development') it('omits stack trace in response when NODE_ENV=production') it('sanitizes error message in production for 500s (returns generic message)') it('passes status code through when err.statusCode is set') describe('errorHandler — integration via /dev/error route') it('GET /dev/error returns 500 with { error, code } body') it('GET /dev/error?status=422 returns 422') it('GET /unknown-route returns 404 JSON with { error, code }') it('404 response Content-Type is application/json') it('500 response Content-Type is application/json') ``` ### Implementation: `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` Key design decisions: - Export two separate middleware functions: `notFoundHandler` (4-arg catch-all returning 404) and `errorHandler` (5-arg Express error handler). - Import `NotFoundError`, `DatabaseError`, `ForeignKeyError` from `../db/queries.js` to do `instanceof` mapping. - Map error types to HTTP status + `code` string: | Error class / condition | HTTP status | `code` | |---|---|---| | `NotFoundError` | 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | | `ForeignKeyError` | 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | | `DatabaseError` | 500 | `DATABASE_ERROR` | | `err.status === 400` or validation | 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | | `err.status === 401` | 401 | `UNAUTHORIZED` | | `err.status === 403` | 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | | Generic `Error` | 500 | `INTERNAL_ERROR` | - In development (`NODE_ENV !== 'production'`): log full stack to `console.error`, include `details: { stack }` in the JSON response. - In production: log `[ERROR] ${code} ${status} ${err.message}` without stack; omit `details` from response; for 500s return a generic `"Internal Server Error"` message (do not leak internal messages). - Response shape: `{ error: string, code: string, details?: object }`. ### Wiring into `server/index.js` Replace the inline `404` and error handlers at the bottom of `createApp()` with: ```javascript import { notFoundHandler, errorHandler } from './middleware/errorHandler.js'; // ... app.use(notFoundHandler); app.use(errorHandler); ``` The existing `app.use((_req, res) => res.status(404).json({ error: 'Not Found' }))` and `app.use((err, _req, res, _next) => ...)` are removed. **Backward compatibility**: All existing tests assert `typeof body.error === 'string'` — the new shape satisfies this because it still has an `error` string field. ### How to run ```bash node --test server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs # Or in full suite: npm -w server run test ``` --- ## Subtask 2: Logging with Morgan ### New files - `server/middleware/logger.js` — application logger utility + request-ID middleware - `server/test/logger.test.mjs` — test suite ### Test file: `server/test/logger.test.mjs` ``` describe('morgan HTTP logging middleware — integration') it('is present in dev mode (NODE_ENV != production)') it('logs requests to stdout in dev format (smoke test — does not throw)') it('is replaced with combined format in production mode') describe('request ID middleware') it('attaches X-Request-Id header to every response') it('X-Request-Id is a non-empty string') it('each request gets a unique X-Request-Id') it('if incoming request has X-Request-Id, it is echoed back') describe('application logger utility') it('logger.info(msg) writes to stdout with timestamp and level INFO') it('logger.error(msg) writes to stderr with timestamp and level ERROR') it('logger.warn(msg) writes to stdout with timestamp and level WARN') it('logger.debug(msg) does not write in production') it('logger.debug(msg) writes in development') ``` ### Implementation: `server/middleware/logger.js` ```javascript // Exports: // 1. morganMiddleware — returns the configured morgan instance // dev: morgan('dev') // production: morgan('combined', { stream: fileStream or stdout }) // 2. requestIdMiddleware — attaches req.id and sets X-Request-Id header // 3. logger — { info, warn, error, debug } with ISO timestamp prefix ``` Design notes: - `requestIdMiddleware` checks `req.headers['x-request-id']` first; falls back to `crypto.randomUUID()` (Node 19+ built-in, or `uuid` which is already a dependency). - `logger` is a plain object with arrow functions, no class needed. - `logger.debug` checks `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'` before writing. - The `morganMiddleware` is a factory function that reads `process.env.NODE_ENV` at call time so tests can control the environment. ### Wiring into `server/index.js` ```javascript import { morganMiddleware, requestIdMiddleware } from './middleware/logger.js'; // Add immediately after app = express(): app.use(requestIdMiddleware); app.use(morganMiddleware()); ``` ### How to run ```bash node --test server/test/logger.test.mjs ``` --- ## Subtask 3: Rate Limiting and Security ### New files - `server/middleware/security.js` — helmet + rate limiters - `server/test/security.test.mjs` — test suite ### Test file: `server/test/security.test.mjs` ``` describe('helmet security headers') it('sets X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff') it('sets X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN or DENY') it('sets X-DNS-Prefetch-Control') it('removes X-Powered-By header') describe('general API rate limiter (100 req / 15 min window)') it('allows requests under the limit') it('returns 429 when limit is exceeded') it('429 response has Retry-After header') it('429 response body has { error, code } shape with code RATE_LIMITED') it('429 response has RateLimit-* headers (or X-RateLimit-* depending on config)') describe('write operation rate limiter (stricter limit)') it('applies stricter limit to POST /api/cards') it('applies stricter limit to DELETE /api/cards/:id') it('applies stricter limit to PATCH /api/cards/:id') it('does NOT apply stricter limit to GET /api/cards') describe('JSON body size limit') it('rejects body larger than 100kb with 413 status') it('accepts body at or below 100kb') ``` ### Implementation: `server/middleware/security.js` ```javascript import helmet from 'helmet'; import rateLimit from 'express-rate-limit'; export const helmetMiddleware = helmet(); // General limiter: 100 req per 15 min export const generalLimiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, max: 100, standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false, handler: (req, res) => { res.status(429).json({ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }); }, }); // Write limiter: 30 req per 15 min for mutating operations export const writeLimiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, max: 30, standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false, handler: (req, res) => { res.status(429).json({ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }); }, }); ``` The `writeLimiter` is applied selectively on POST, PATCH, DELETE routes — not as a global middleware. This is done in `server/index.js` or in the router files. Testing strategy for rate limits: In tests, the `max` value is overridden by creating test-specific limiter instances with `max: 2` so the limit is reachable without making 100 requests. The test imports the factory from `security.js` rather than the singleton, or `security.js` exports a factory. Alternative (simpler for testing): export `createRateLimiter(options)` factory so tests can create low-limit instances: ```javascript export function createRateLimiter(options) { return rateLimit({ standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false, ...options }); } ``` ### JSON body size limit `express.json()` already accepts a `limit` option. In `server/index.js`, change: ```javascript app.use(express.json()); // to: app.use(express.json({ limit: '100kb' })); ``` Express sends a 413 automatically when the body exceeds the limit, and the error handler catches it (Express sets `err.status = 413` on the SyntaxError it generates). ### Wiring into `server/index.js` ```javascript import { helmetMiddleware, generalLimiter, writeLimiter } from './middleware/security.js'; app.use(helmetMiddleware); app.use('/api', generalLimiter); // Write limiter applied to mutating routes: app.use('/api/cards', writeLimiter); // covers POST /api/cards // PATCH and DELETE on /api/cards/:id need selective application in the router or a method check ``` The cleanest approach: apply `writeLimiter` directly in the route definitions in `cards.js` and `comments.js` as route-level middleware (`router.post('/cards', writeLimiter, handler)`), keeping `server/index.js` clean. ### How to run ```bash node --test server/test/security.test.mjs ``` --- ## Subtask 6: Production CORS and Health Checks (Placed before client subtasks because it is a server concern and completes the server work.) ### Files modified / created - `server/index.js` — update CORS config, enhance `/health`, add `/health/ready`, `/health/live`, `/metrics` - `server/test/health.test.mjs` — new test file ### Test file: `server/test/health.test.mjs` ``` describe('GET /health (basic liveness + DB check)') it('returns 200 with { ok: true } when DB is up') it('returns { ok: true, db: true } when DB is accessible') it('returns 503 with { ok: false, db: false } when DB is unavailable') it('response has Content-Type: application/json') describe('GET /health/live (liveness probe)') it('returns 200 with { status: "ok" }') it('does not check DB (always fast)') it('response has Content-Type: application/json') describe('GET /health/ready (readiness probe)') it('returns 200 with { status: "ready", db: true } when DB accessible') it('returns 503 with { status: "unavailable", db: false } when DB down') it('includes ws field indicating WebSocket server state') describe('GET /metrics') it('returns 200') it('response includes uptime (number, >= 0)') it('response includes memory { heapUsed, heapTotal, rss } fields') it('response includes requestCount (number)') it('requestCount increments with each request') describe('CORS production configuration') it('in production with CORS_ORIGIN set, allows that origin') it('in production without CORS_ORIGIN, blocks cross-origin requests') it('in development, reflects any origin') it('CORS_ORIGIN supports comma-separated list of origins') ``` ### Implementation changes in `server/index.js` **CORS update:** ```javascript const corsOrigin = (() => { if (!isProduction) return true; // reflect any origin in dev const env = process.env.CORS_ORIGIN; if (!env) return false; const origins = env.split(',').map(o => o.trim()); return origins.length === 1 ? origins[0] : origins; })(); app.use(cors({ origin: corsOrigin, credentials: true })); ``` **Request counter** (for `/metrics`): a module-level `let requestCount = 0` incremented by a middleware registered before routes. Can be exported from `logger.js` or kept in `index.js`. **Enhanced `/health`:** ```javascript app.get('/health', (_req, res) => { const db = getDb(); let dbOk = false; try { db?.prepare('SELECT 1').get(); dbOk = true; } catch { /* db down */ } if (!dbOk) return res.status(503).json({ ok: false, db: false }); return res.json({ ok: true, db: true }); }); ``` **Liveness probe** (stateless, always fast): ```javascript app.get('/health/live', (_req, res) => res.json({ status: 'ok' })); ``` **Readiness probe** (checks DB + WS): ```javascript app.get('/health/ready', (_req, res) => { const db = getDb(); let dbOk = false; try { db?.prepare('SELECT 1').get(); dbOk = true; } catch { /* */ } const wsOk = getWsState(); // from broadcaster.js — export a getter if (!dbOk) return res.status(503).json({ status: 'unavailable', db: false, ws: wsOk }); return res.json({ status: 'ready', db: true, ws: wsOk }); }); ``` This requires adding a `getWsState()` export to `server/ws/broadcaster.js` returning `boolean` (whether `wss !== null`). **Metrics endpoint:** ```javascript app.get('/metrics', (_req, res) => { const mem = process.memoryUsage(); res.json({ uptime: process.uptime(), memory: { heapUsed: mem.heapUsed, heapTotal: mem.heapTotal, rss: mem.rss }, requestCount, }); }); ``` ### How to run ```bash node --test server/test/health.test.mjs ``` --- ## Subtask 4: Client Bundle Code Splitting ### Files modified / created - `client/vite.config.js` — add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` - `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` — replace static `CardModal` import with `React.lazy`; add `Suspense` boundary - `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` — update mocks for lazy `CardModal` - `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` — new spinner/skeleton component - `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx` — test the spinner ### Test file approach The primary concern is that the existing `Board.test.jsx` continues to pass after introducing `React.lazy`. The mock strategy is: In `Board.test.jsx`, `CardModal` is not directly imported — it is rendered by `Board`. Since `vi.mock()` applies to the module graph, add: ```javascript vi.mock('./CardModal.jsx', () => ({ default: ({ card, onClose }) =>
})) ``` This mock replaces the lazy-loaded module transparently — Vitest handles `React.lazy` the same as a regular import when the module is mocked. **New test file: `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx`** ``` describe('LoadingSpinner') it('renders without crashing') it('has accessible role="status" or aria-label="Loading"') it('accepts a size prop (sm, md, lg)') it('accepts a label prop for custom aria-label') describe('SkeletonBoard (loading fallback)') it('renders placeholder column shapes') it('does not render real card content') ``` **New test file: `client/src/components/Board/Board.lazy.test.jsx`** (verifies the lazy/Suspense integration): ``` describe('Board with lazy CardModal') it('renders the board without opening modal (no Suspense triggered)') it('shows Suspense fallback while CardModal chunk is loading') // achieved by wrapping React.lazy(() => new Promise that never resolves) // and checking that LoadingSpinner appears it('renders modal after lazy load completes') ``` In Vitest, `React.lazy` resolves synchronously when the dynamic import resolves synchronously (because `vi.mock()` is synchronous). The loading fallback test requires a controlled lazy factory: ```javascript // Controlled lazy for testing suspension const ControlledModal = React.lazy(() => { return new Promise(() => {}) // never resolves → stays in Suspense }) ``` ### Implementation: `client/vite.config.js` Add inside `defineConfig`: ```javascript build: { rollupOptions: { output: { manualChunks: { 'vendor-react': ['react', 'react-dom'], 'vendor-dnd': ['@dnd-kit/core', '@dnd-kit/sortable', '@dnd-kit/utilities'], 'vendor-blocknote': ['@blocknote/core', '@blocknote/react'], }, }, }, }, ``` ### Implementation: `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` Change the static import: ```javascript // Before: import CardModal from './CardModal.jsx' // After: import { lazy, Suspense } from 'react' import LoadingSpinner from '../LoadingSpinner.jsx' const CardModal = lazy(() => import('./CardModal.jsx')) ``` Wrap the `CardModal` JSX in a `Suspense`: ```jsx {selectedCard && ( }> setSelectedCardId(null)} onUpdate={updateCard} onDelete={deleteCard} onAddComment={addComment} /> )} ``` ### Implementation: `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` ```jsx export default function LoadingSpinner({ label = 'Loading', size = 'md' }) { return (
{label}
) } ``` ### How to run ```bash npm -w client run test # For build verification: npm -w client run build ``` --- ## Subtask 5: React Error Boundaries and Loading States ### New files / modified files - `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` — class component error boundary - `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` — test suite - `client/src/App.jsx` — wrap `` in `` - `client/src/App.test.jsx` — add tests for error boundary behavior in App ### Test file: `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` ``` describe('ErrorBoundary') it('renders children when there is no error') it('renders fallback UI when child throws during render') it('fallback shows an error heading') it('fallback shows a "Try again" / retry button') it('clicking retry clears the error boundary and re-renders children') it('clicking retry calls onReset prop if provided') it('catches errors from deeply nested children') it('calls onError prop with the error and errorInfo when an error is caught') it('renders custom fallback component when fallback prop is a component') describe('ErrorBoundary in App context') it('renders app normally when Board renders without errors') it('shows error boundary fallback when Board throws') it('retry button causes re-render attempt') ``` The test strategy for triggering errors in RTL: create a `ThrowOnRender` helper component: ```jsx function ThrowOnRender({ shouldThrow }) { if (shouldThrow) throw new Error('Test render error') return
OK
} ``` Vitest captures the `console.error` calls from React's error reporting using `vi.spyOn(console, 'error').mockImplementation(() => {})` in `beforeEach` to suppress noise. ### Implementation: `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` Class component (required — hooks cannot be used in error boundaries): ```jsx import { Component } from 'react' export default class ErrorBoundary extends Component { constructor(props) { super(props) this.state = { hasError: false, error: null } this.handleReset = this.handleReset.bind(this) } static getDerivedStateFromError(error) { return { hasError: true, error } } componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) { this.props.onError?.(error, errorInfo) // In production: could send to monitoring service here if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') { console.error('[ErrorBoundary]', error, errorInfo) } } handleReset() { this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null }) this.props.onReset?.() } render() { if (this.state.hasError) { if (this.props.fallback) { const Fallback = this.props.fallback return } return (

Something went wrong

{this.state.error?.message}

) } return this.props.children } } ``` ### Implementation: `client/src/App.jsx` ```jsx import ErrorBoundary from './components/ErrorBoundary.jsx' import Board from './components/Board/Board.jsx' import './App.css' function App() { return (

Kanban Board

) } export default App ``` ### Improved loading states Board.jsx currently renders `
Loading…
`. Replace with: ```jsx if (loading) return ``` This requires `LoadingSpinner` (created in Subtask 4) to be available. Subtask 5 thus has a soft dependency on Subtask 4's `LoadingSpinner`. Board.test.jsx already tests `screen.getByLabelText('Loading')` — this continues to pass since `LoadingSpinner` renders `aria-label={label}`. ### How to run ```bash npm -w client run test ``` --- ## Complete Test Running Reference ```bash # Run all server tests (existing + new): npm -w server run test:all # Run individual new test suites: node --test kanban/server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs node --test kanban/server/test/logger.test.mjs node --test kanban/server/test/security.test.mjs node --test kanban/server/test/health.test.mjs # Run all client tests: npm -w client run test # Run specific client test file: npx vitest run client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx npx vitest run client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx # Run everything from the root: npm run test:all # Run with coverage: npm run test:coverage ``` --- ## Critical Compatibility Notes 1. **Existing `body.error` tests**: every existing route test asserts `typeof body.error === 'string'`. The new `{ error, code, details? }` shape preserves this — no changes needed to existing tests. 2. **`GET /health` existing test** in `server.test.mjs` asserts `deepStrictEqual(body, { ok: true })`. The enhanced health endpoint adds a `db` field, making this test fail. Two options: - **Option A (preferred)**: Change the test assertion from `deepStrictEqual` to check `body.ok === true` — but this is a modification to an existing test, which must be done alongside the subtask. - **Option B**: Keep a separate `/health/legacy` that returns exactly `{ ok: true }` — inelegant. Go with **Option A**: the existing test `'returns { ok: true }'` should become `assert.ok(body.ok)` or `assert.strictEqual(body.ok, true)`. The new `health.test.mjs` suite covers the full enhanced behavior. Document this as an intentional breaking change to the test, part of the subtask. 3. **Morgan in tests**: Morgan writes to stdout during tests, which creates noise. Suppress it in the test environment by checking `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test'` and skipping the morgan middleware, or by passing a `skip` option to morgan: ```javascript morgan('dev', { skip: () => process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test' }) ``` 4. **Rate limiter in tests**: The general limiter at 100 req/15 min will not be hit during normal test runs. The `security.test.mjs` creates test-specific low-limit instances using the exported factory function. 5. **`React.lazy` in Vitest**: Vitest supports dynamic imports natively. When `vi.mock('./CardModal.jsx', ...)` is called, `React.lazy(() => import('./CardModal.jsx'))` resolves synchronously to the mock, so existing Board tests that open the modal continue to work without modification. 6. **`ErrorBoundary` and React StrictMode**: React 18 in StrictMode calls `getDerivedStateFromError` / `componentDidCatch` twice in development. Tests should either wrap in a non-strict container or suppress the double-invocation noise with `vi.spyOn(console, 'error').mockImplementation`. --- ### Critical Files for Implementation - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-24/kanban/server/index.js` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-24/kanban/server/db/queries.js` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-24/kanban/client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-24/kanban/client/vite.config.js` - `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-24/kanban/server/test/server.test.mjs`

Design a detailed TDD implementation plan for adding production optimizations and error handling to a Kanban board app. Here is what I know about the codebase:

## Project Location
`/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-24/kanban/`

## Server Structure
- `server/index.js` — Main Express app, uses ESM (`type: "module"` in package.json)
- `server/api/cards.js` — Card routes
- `server/api/comments.js` — Comment routes
- `server/db/queries.js` — Database layer with custom error classes: `NotFoundError`, `DatabaseError`, `ForeignKeyError`
- `server/ws/broadcaster.js` — WebSocket broadcaster
- Tests use Node's native `node:test` module + `supertest`
- Test files are `.mjs` (ESM) in `server/test/`
- Existing test helpers: `startTestServer()`, `stopServer()` in server/test/server.test.mjs

## Current Server Middleware (server/index.js)
```javascript
// CORS
app.use(cors({
  origin: isProduction ? false : true,
  credentials: true,
}));
app.use(express.json());

// Routes
app.use('/api', commentsRouter);
app.use('/api', cardsRouter);

// Health check
app.get('/health', (_req, res) => res.json({ ok: true }));

// Dev-only routes
if (!isProduction) {
  app.post('/dev/echo', (req, res) => res.json(req.body));
  app.get('/dev/error', (_req, res, next) => next(new Error('deliberate error')));
}

// Static serving in production
if (isProduction) {
  app.use(express.static(path.join(rootDir, '../client/dist')));
  app.get('*', (_req, res) => res.sendFile(...));
}

// 404 handler
app.use((_req, res) => res.status(404).json({ error: 'Not Found' }));

// Error handler
app.use((err, _req, res, _next) => {
  const status = err.status || err.statusCode || 500;
  res.status(status).json({ error: err.message || 'Internal Server Error' });
});
```

## Client Structure
- `client/src/main.jsx` — React entry (StrictMode)
- `client/src/App.jsx` — Root component (renders Board)
- `client/vite.config.js` — Vite with Vitest config, proxies `/api` and `/ws` to port 3001
- No React Router — single-page app with portal-based modal
- No lazy loading or error boundaries yet
- Loading states exist in components (using `loading`/`error` flags in useBoard hook)
- Tests use Vitest + React Testing Library

## Task Requirements (6 Subtasks)

### Subtask 1: Comprehensive Error Handling Middleware
- Create `server/middleware/errorHandler.js`
- Handle: NotFoundError→404, ForeignKeyError→404, DatabaseError→500, validation→400, auth→401/403, generic→500
- Log with stack traces in dev, sanitized in production
- Consistent response format: `{ error: string, code: string, [details]: any }`
- 404 handler for unmatched routes

### Subtask 2: Logging with Morgan
- Install `morgan`
- Dev: `dev` format to stdout
- Production: `combined` format, optionally to file stream  
- Custom application logger utility (timestamps, log levels)
- Request ID middleware (attach unique ID to each request)

### Subtask 3: Rate Limiting and Security
- Install `express-rate-limit` and `helmet`
- Rate limits: general API (100/15min window), stricter for write operations
- Helmet security headers
- Retry-After header in 429 responses
- Request size limits (already handled by express.json but add limits)

### Subtask 4: Client Bundle Code Splitting
- Update `vite.config.js` for code splitting (manualChunks for vendor libs)
- Implement React.lazy() for CardModal (heavy - contains BlockEditor with BlockNote)
- Add Suspense boundaries with loading fallbacks
- The BlockEditor is already only used inside CardModal

### Subtask 5: React Error Boundaries and Loading States
- Create `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx`
- Wrap App or Board in ErrorBoundary
- Add Suspense boundary for lazy-loaded CardModal
- Improve loading UI (skeleton or spinner component)
- Error fallback with retry button

### Subtask 6: Production CORS and Health Checks
- Update CORS config: allow `CORS_ORIGIN` env var for production origins
- Enhance `/health` endpoint: test DB connectivity, check WS server
- Add `/health/ready` (readiness probe) and `/health/live` (liveness probe)
- Add basic `/metrics` endpoint (uptime, memory, request count)

## Constraints
- Maintain ESM module syntax throughout
- Server tests use `node:test` + `supertest`, not Jest/Vitest
- Client tests use Vitest + React Testing Library
- Follow TDD: write tests FIRST, then implementation
- Keep existing tests passing

Please design a detailed implementation plan specifying:
1. For each subtask: exact test file, test case names, implementation file(s), specific code patterns
2. Order of execution
3. Dependencies between subtasks
4. Any new packages to install
5. How to run tests for each subtask

content.text:
Now I have everything I need to produce a comprehensive implementation plan. Here it is: --- # TDD Implementation Plan: Production Optimizations and Error Handling ## Preamble: Conventions Observed in the Codebase Before the subtask plans, several conventions must be respected throughout: - **Server tests**: `.mjs` files in `server/test/`, using `node:test` + `node:assert/strict` + native `fetch`. No `supertest`. Each suite manages its own `startTestServer` / `stopServer` helpers (defined locally in the file). Tests run with `node --test`. - **Server modules**: ESM (`type: "module"`). All new server files use `import`/`export`, never `require`. - **Client tests**: `.test.jsx` or `.test.js` in co-located `src/` directories, using Vitest + React Testing Library. `vi.mock()` for all hooks and heavy dependencies. - **Error response shape**: existing routes return `{ error: string }`. The plan enriches this to `{ error: string, code: string, details?: any }` while keeping the `error` field, so all existing test assertions (`assert.strictEqual(typeof body.error, 'string')`) continue to pass. - **Middleware directory**: `server/middleware/` does not yet exist and must be created as part of the work. --- ## Execution Order and Dependencies ``` Subtask 1 (Error Handler Middleware) → Subtask 2 (Morgan / Logger) depends on S1 being stable → Subtask 3 (Rate Limiting / Helmet) depends on S1 and S2 being in place → Subtask 6 (CORS / Health Checks) is partially independent; DB health check needs Subtask 1 done Subtask 4 (Vite Code Splitting) — fully independent of S1–S3 Subtask 5 (React Error Boundaries) — fully independent of S1–S4, but logically pairs with S4 ``` Recommended order: **1 → 2 → 3 → 6 → 4 → 5** Subtasks 4 and 5 are purely client-side and can be done in parallel with the server subtasks if there are multiple contributors. --- ## New Packages to Install ### Server (`kanban/server/`) ``` npm -w server install morgan express-rate-limit helmet ``` Add to `server/package.json` `dependencies`: - `morgan` — HTTP request logger middleware - `express-rate-limit` — Rate limiting - `helmet` — Security headers No new `devDependencies` needed for the server; the existing `node:test` + native `fetch` covers all new tests. ### Client (`kanban/client/`) No new packages needed. `React.lazy` + `Suspense` are built into React 18. The `ErrorBoundary` is a class component using existing React APIs. --- ## Subtask 1: Comprehensive Error Handling Middleware ### New files - `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` — the error handler middleware - `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` — the test suite ### Test file: `server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs` Structure using `node:test`: ``` describe('errorHandler middleware — unit') it('maps NotFoundError to 404 with code NOT_FOUND') it('maps ForeignKeyError to 404 with code NOT_FOUND') it('maps DatabaseError to 500 with code DATABASE_ERROR') it('maps validation error (err.status=400) to 400 with code VALIDATION_ERROR') it('maps auth error (err.status=401) to 401 with code UNAUTHORIZED') it('maps auth error (err.status=403) to 403 with code FORBIDDEN') it('maps generic Error to 500 with code INTERNAL_ERROR') it('response body always has { error: string, code: string } shape') it('includes stack trace in response details when NODE_ENV=development') it('omits stack trace in response when NODE_ENV=production') it('sanitizes error message in production for 500s (returns generic message)') it('passes status code through when err.statusCode is set') describe('errorHandler — integration via /dev/error route') it('GET /dev/error returns 500 with { error, code } body') it('GET /dev/error?status=422 returns 422') it('GET /unknown-route returns 404 JSON with { error, code }') it('404 response Content-Type is application/json') it('500 response Content-Type is application/json') ``` ### Implementation: `server/middleware/errorHandler.js` Key design decisions: - Export two separate middleware functions: `notFoundHandler` (4-arg catch-all returning 404) and `errorHandler` (5-arg Express error handler). - Import `NotFoundError`, `DatabaseError`, `ForeignKeyError` from `../db/queries.js` to do `instanceof` mapping. - Map error types to HTTP status + `code` string: | Error class / condition | HTTP status | `code` | |---|---|---| | `NotFoundError` | 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | | `ForeignKeyError` | 404 | `NOT_FOUND` | | `DatabaseError` | 500 | `DATABASE_ERROR` | | `err.status === 400` or validation | 400 | `VALIDATION_ERROR` | | `err.status === 401` | 401 | `UNAUTHORIZED` | | `err.status === 403` | 403 | `FORBIDDEN` | | Generic `Error` | 500 | `INTERNAL_ERROR` | - In development (`NODE_ENV !== 'production'`): log full stack to `console.error`, include `details: { stack }` in the JSON response. - In production: log `[ERROR] ${code} ${status} ${err.message}` without stack; omit `details` from response; for 500s return a generic `"Internal Server Error"` message (do not leak internal messages). - Response shape: `{ error: string, code: string, details?: object }`. ### Wiring into `server/index.js` Replace the inline `404` and error handlers at the bottom of `createApp()` with: ```javascript import { notFoundHandler, errorHandler } from './middleware/errorHandler.js'; // ... app.use(notFoundHandler); app.use(errorHandler); ``` The existing `app.use((_req, res) => res.status(404).json({ error: 'Not Found' }))` and `app.use((err, _req, res, _next) => ...)` are removed. **Backward compatibility**: All existing tests assert `typeof body.error === 'string'` — the new shape satisfies this because it still has an `error` string field. ### How to run ```bash node --test server/test/errorHandler.test.mjs # Or in full suite: npm -w server run test ``` --- ## Subtask 2: Logging with Morgan ### New files - `server/middleware/logger.js` — application logger utility + request-ID middleware - `server/test/logger.test.mjs` — test suite ### Test file: `server/test/logger.test.mjs` ``` describe('morgan HTTP logging middleware — integration') it('is present in dev mode (NODE_ENV != production)') it('logs requests to stdout in dev format (smoke test — does not throw)') it('is replaced with combined format in production mode') describe('request ID middleware') it('attaches X-Request-Id header to every response') it('X-Request-Id is a non-empty string') it('each request gets a unique X-Request-Id') it('if incoming request has X-Request-Id, it is echoed back') describe('application logger utility') it('logger.info(msg) writes to stdout with timestamp and level INFO') it('logger.error(msg) writes to stderr with timestamp and level ERROR') it('logger.warn(msg) writes to stdout with timestamp and level WARN') it('logger.debug(msg) does not write in production') it('logger.debug(msg) writes in development') ``` ### Implementation: `server/middleware/logger.js` ```javascript // Exports: // 1. morganMiddleware — returns the configured morgan instance // dev: morgan('dev') // production: morgan('combined', { stream: fileStream or stdout }) // 2. requestIdMiddleware — attaches req.id and sets X-Request-Id header // 3. logger — { info, warn, error, debug } with ISO timestamp prefix ``` Design notes: - `requestIdMiddleware` checks `req.headers['x-request-id']` first; falls back to `crypto.randomUUID()` (Node 19+ built-in, or `uuid` which is already a dependency). - `logger` is a plain object with arrow functions, no class needed. - `logger.debug` checks `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'` before writing. - The `morganMiddleware` is a factory function that reads `process.env.NODE_ENV` at call time so tests can control the environment. ### Wiring into `server/index.js` ```javascript import { morganMiddleware, requestIdMiddleware } from './middleware/logger.js'; // Add immediately after app = express(): app.use(requestIdMiddleware); app.use(morganMiddleware()); ``` ### How to run ```bash node --test server/test/logger.test.mjs ``` --- ## Subtask 3: Rate Limiting and Security ### New files - `server/middleware/security.js` — helmet + rate limiters - `server/test/security.test.mjs` — test suite ### Test file: `server/test/security.test.mjs` ``` describe('helmet security headers') it('sets X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff') it('sets X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN or DENY') it('sets X-DNS-Prefetch-Control') it('removes X-Powered-By header') describe('general API rate limiter (100 req / 15 min window)') it('allows requests under the limit') it('returns 429 when limit is exceeded') it('429 response has Retry-After header') it('429 response body has { error, code } shape with code RATE_LIMITED') it('429 response has RateLimit-* headers (or X-RateLimit-* depending on config)') describe('write operation rate limiter (stricter limit)') it('applies stricter limit to POST /api/cards') it('applies stricter limit to DELETE /api/cards/:id') it('applies stricter limit to PATCH /api/cards/:id') it('does NOT apply stricter limit to GET /api/cards') describe('JSON body size limit') it('rejects body larger than 100kb with 413 status') it('accepts body at or below 100kb') ``` ### Implementation: `server/middleware/security.js` ```javascript import helmet from 'helmet'; import rateLimit from 'express-rate-limit'; export const helmetMiddleware = helmet(); // General limiter: 100 req per 15 min export const generalLimiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, max: 100, standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false, handler: (req, res) => { res.status(429).json({ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }); }, }); // Write limiter: 30 req per 15 min for mutating operations export const writeLimiter = rateLimit({ windowMs: 15 * 60 * 1000, max: 30, standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false, handler: (req, res) => { res.status(429).json({ error: 'Too many requests', code: 'RATE_LIMITED' }); }, }); ``` The `writeLimiter` is applied selectively on POST, PATCH, DELETE routes — not as a global middleware. This is done in `server/index.js` or in the router files. Testing strategy for rate limits: In tests, the `max` value is overridden by creating test-specific limiter instances with `max: 2` so the limit is reachable without making 100 requests. The test imports the factory from `security.js` rather than the singleton, or `security.js` exports a factory. Alternative (simpler for testing): export `createRateLimiter(options)` factory so tests can create low-limit instances: ```javascript export function createRateLimiter(options) { return rateLimit({ standardHeaders: true, legacyHeaders: false, ...options }); } ``` ### JSON body size limit `express.json()` already accepts a `limit` option. In `server/index.js`, change: ```javascript app.use(express.json()); // to: app.use(express.json({ limit: '100kb' })); ``` Express sends a 413 automatically when the body exceeds the limit, and the error handler catches it (Express sets `err.status = 413` on the SyntaxError it generates). ### Wiring into `server/index.js` ```javascript import { helmetMiddleware, generalLimiter, writeLimiter } from './middleware/security.js'; app.use(helmetMiddleware); app.use('/api', generalLimiter); // Write limiter applied to mutating routes: app.use('/api/cards', writeLimiter); // covers POST /api/cards // PATCH and DELETE on /api/cards/:id need selective application in the router or a method check ``` The cleanest approach: apply `writeLimiter` directly in the route definitions in `cards.js` and `comments.js` as route-level middleware (`router.post('/cards', writeLimiter, handler)`), keeping `server/index.js` clean. ### How to run ```bash node --test server/test/security.test.mjs ``` --- ## Subtask 6: Production CORS and Health Checks (Placed before client subtasks because it is a server concern and completes the server work.) ### Files modified / created - `server/index.js` — update CORS config, enhance `/health`, add `/health/ready`, `/health/live`, `/metrics` - `server/test/health.test.mjs` — new test file ### Test file: `server/test/health.test.mjs` ``` describe('GET /health (basic liveness + DB check)') it('returns 200 with { ok: true } when DB is up') it('returns { ok: true, db: true } when DB is accessible') it('returns 503 with { ok: false, db: false } when DB is unavailable') it('response has Content-Type: application/json') describe('GET /health/live (liveness probe)') it('returns 200 with { status: "ok" }') it('does not check DB (always fast)') it('response has Content-Type: application/json') describe('GET /health/ready (readiness probe)') it('returns 200 with { status: "ready", db: true } when DB accessible') it('returns 503 with { status: "unavailable", db: false } when DB down') it('includes ws field indicating WebSocket server state') describe('GET /metrics') it('returns 200') it('response includes uptime (number, >= 0)') it('response includes memory { heapUsed, heapTotal, rss } fields') it('response includes requestCount (number)') it('requestCount increments with each request') describe('CORS production configuration') it('in production with CORS_ORIGIN set, allows that origin') it('in production without CORS_ORIGIN, blocks cross-origin requests') it('in development, reflects any origin') it('CORS_ORIGIN supports comma-separated list of origins') ``` ### Implementation changes in `server/index.js` **CORS update:** ```javascript const corsOrigin = (() => { if (!isProduction) return true; // reflect any origin in dev const env = process.env.CORS_ORIGIN; if (!env) return false; const origins = env.split(',').map(o => o.trim()); return origins.length === 1 ? origins[0] : origins; })(); app.use(cors({ origin: corsOrigin, credentials: true })); ``` **Request counter** (for `/metrics`): a module-level `let requestCount = 0` incremented by a middleware registered before routes. Can be exported from `logger.js` or kept in `index.js`. **Enhanced `/health`:** ```javascript app.get('/health', (_req, res) => { const db = getDb(); let dbOk = false; try { db?.prepare('SELECT 1').get(); dbOk = true; } catch { /* db down */ } if (!dbOk) return res.status(503).json({ ok: false, db: false }); return res.json({ ok: true, db: true }); }); ``` **Liveness probe** (stateless, always fast): ```javascript app.get('/health/live', (_req, res) => res.json({ status: 'ok' })); ``` **Readiness probe** (checks DB + WS): ```javascript app.get('/health/ready', (_req, res) => { const db = getDb(); let dbOk = false; try { db?.prepare('SELECT 1').get(); dbOk = true; } catch { /* */ } const wsOk = getWsState(); // from broadcaster.js — export a getter if (!dbOk) return res.status(503).json({ status: 'unavailable', db: false, ws: wsOk }); return res.json({ status: 'ready', db: true, ws: wsOk }); }); ``` This requires adding a `getWsState()` export to `server/ws/broadcaster.js` returning `boolean` (whether `wss !== null`). **Metrics endpoint:** ```javascript app.get('/metrics', (_req, res) => { const mem = process.memoryUsage(); res.json({ uptime: process.uptime(), memory: { heapUsed: mem.heapUsed, heapTotal: mem.heapTotal, rss: mem.rss }, requestCount, }); }); ``` ### How to run ```bash node --test server/test/health.test.mjs ``` --- ## Subtask 4: Client Bundle Code Splitting ### Files modified / created - `client/vite.config.js` — add `build.rollupOptions.output.manualChunks` - `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` — replace static `CardModal` import with `React.lazy`; add `Suspense` boundary - `client/src/components/Board/Board.test.jsx` — update mocks for lazy `CardModal` - `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` — new spinner/skeleton component - `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx` — test the spinner ### Test file approach The primary concern is that the existing `Board.test.jsx` continues to pass after introducing `React.lazy`. The mock strategy is: In `Board.test.jsx`, `CardModal` is not directly imported — it is rendered by `Board`. Since `vi.mock()` applies to the module graph, add: ```javascript vi.mock('./CardModal.jsx', () => ({ default: ({ card, onClose }) =>
})) ``` This mock replaces the lazy-loaded module transparently — Vitest handles `React.lazy` the same as a regular import when the module is mocked. **New test file: `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.test.jsx`** ``` describe('LoadingSpinner') it('renders without crashing') it('has accessible role="status" or aria-label="Loading"') it('accepts a size prop (sm, md, lg)') it('accepts a label prop for custom aria-label') describe('SkeletonBoard (loading fallback)') it('renders placeholder column shapes') it('does not render real card content') ``` **New test file: `client/src/components/Board/Board.lazy.test.jsx`** (verifies the lazy/Suspense integration): ``` describe('Board with lazy CardModal') it('renders the board without opening modal (no Suspense triggered)') it('shows Suspense fallback while CardModal chunk is loading') // achieved by wrapping React.lazy(() => new Promise that never resolves) // and checking that LoadingSpinner appears it('renders modal after lazy load completes') ``` In Vitest, `React.lazy` resolves synchronously when the dynamic import resolves synchronously (because `vi.mock()` is synchronous). The loading fallback test requires a controlled lazy factory: ```javascript // Controlled lazy for testing suspension const ControlledModal = React.lazy(() => { return new Promise(() => {}) // never resolves → stays in Suspense }) ``` ### Implementation: `client/vite.config.js` Add inside `defineConfig`: ```javascript build: { rollupOptions: { output: { manualChunks: { 'vendor-react': ['react', 'react-dom'], 'vendor-dnd': ['@dnd-kit/core', '@dnd-kit/sortable', '@dnd-kit/utilities'], 'vendor-blocknote': ['@blocknote/core', '@blocknote/react'], }, }, }, }, ``` ### Implementation: `client/src/components/Board/Board.jsx` Change the static import: ```javascript // Before: import CardModal from './CardModal.jsx' // After: import { lazy, Suspense } from 'react' import LoadingSpinner from '../LoadingSpinner.jsx' const CardModal = lazy(() => import('./CardModal.jsx')) ``` Wrap the `CardModal` JSX in a `Suspense`: ```jsx {selectedCard && ( }> setSelectedCardId(null)} onUpdate={updateCard} onDelete={deleteCard} onAddComment={addComment} /> )} ``` ### Implementation: `client/src/components/LoadingSpinner.jsx` ```jsx export default function LoadingSpinner({ label = 'Loading', size = 'md' }) { return (
{label}
) } ``` ### How to run ```bash npm -w client run test # For build verification: npm -w client run build ``` --- ## Subtask 5: React Error Boundaries and Loading States ### New files / modified files - `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` — class component error boundary - `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` — test suite - `client/src/App.jsx` — wrap `` in `` - `client/src/App.test.jsx` — add tests for error boundary behavior in App ### Test file: `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.test.jsx` ``` describe('ErrorBoundary') it('renders children when there is no error') it('renders fallback UI when child throws during render') it('fallback shows an error heading') it('fallback shows a "Try again" / retry button') it('clicking retry clears the error boundary and re-renders children') it('clicking retry calls onReset prop if provided') it('catches errors from deeply nested children') it('calls onError prop with the error and errorInfo when an error is caught') it('renders custom fallback component when fallback prop is a component') describe('ErrorBoundary in App context') it('renders app normally when Board renders without errors') it('shows error boundary fallback when Board throws') it('retry button causes re-render attempt') ``` The test strategy for triggering errors in RTL: create a `ThrowOnRender` helper component: ```jsx function ThrowOnRender({ shouldThrow }) { if (shouldThrow) throw new Error('Test render error') return
OK
} ``` Vitest captures the `console.error` calls from React's error reporting using `vi.spyOn(console, 'error').mockImplementation(() => {})` in `beforeEach` to suppress noise. ### Implementation: `client/src/components/ErrorBoundary.jsx` Class component (required — hooks cannot be used in error boundaries): ```jsx import { Component } from 'react' export default class ErrorBoundary extends Component { constructor(props) { super(props) this.state = { hasError: false, error: null } this.handleReset = this.handleReset.bind(this) } static getDerivedStateFromError(error) { return { hasError: true, error } } componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) { this.props.onError?.(error, errorInfo) // In production: could send to monitoring service here if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production') { console.error('[ErrorBoundary]', error, errorInfo) } } handleReset() { this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null }) this.props.onReset?.() } render() { if (this.state.hasError) { if (this.props.fallback) { const Fallback = this.props.fallback return } return (
